description_long: This is a medium-sized, twin-engine propeller airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a high-mounted straight wing configuration. The aircraft features a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The engines are mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller, and the landing gear appears to be fixed in a tricycle arrangement. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and is enclosed, likely accommodating one or two crew members. The exterior is finished in a uniform gray color with minimal markings, and the surface appears smooth and metallic. The overall design suggests a utilitarian or surveillance role, suitable for urban or semi-urban air operations.
description: A gray, twin-propeller airplane with high-mounted straight wings, a conventional tail, and a streamlined, enclosed cockpit at the front.
description_view_0: Side view showing the streamlined fuselage, high-mounted wing, and one of the wing-mounted propeller engines.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the twin propellers, symmetrical wing span, and the nose of the airplane.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, showing the other wing and engine, as well as the tail fin.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizers, and the back of the wings and engines.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy, likely accommodating one or two seats.
